summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on fraud/security, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Cash App triggers $45M settlement
articles.description: Block misled consumers about the safety of using the financial app and failed to help users who were fraud victims, 46 states alleged.

articles.title: Fiserv president exits
articles.description: As Dhivya Suryadevara announced her resignation Tuesday, the company elevated two other executives.

articles.title: Fiserv debit network sale skepticism abounds
articles.description: The payment processor reportedly has considered selling a debit network to one of several banks, but many analysts doubt it will happen.

articles.title: Klarna seeks to disrupt US market
articles.description: The Swedish buy now, pay later player aims to shake up financial services, linking with Southwest Air and seeking a bank license this week.

articles.title: How real-time payments expand
articles.description: An early Stripe executive predicts big advances for the growth of consumer-level, real-time payments over the next 18 months.

articles.title: Ex-Bolt executive lands new post
articles.description: The checkout company’s former CEO has become president of the Australian merchant services firm Hello Clever.

articles.title: Digital ID and payments turn to your face
articles.description: As mobile devices and government regulators embrace facial recognition technology, your face is becoming both wallet and identification.

articles.title: The B2B payments infrastructure gap: Most platforms weren’t built for agentic AI
articles.description: Legacy B2B tech stacks were designed to be compatible with automation — not today’s agentic AI.
